behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: The failure is a deterministic bug in request construction. Mock the external API, call the module, and inspect the arguments passed to the mocked function. The complete label list is either in the request or not—no ambiguity. A pure in-process mock will not miss this because you can directly assert on what was sent.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API call. Invoke the classification function. Assert that the mock was called with a dictionary or payload containing all taxonomy labels (e.g., ['legal', 'technical', 'financial', 'other']).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This tests deterministic input validation and branching. Mock the API to return an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., 'invalid_label'). Assert the module returns 'unknown'. A mock will not miss this—you control the API response and observe the output directly.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a label not in the taxonomy. Call the module. Assert that the return value is exactly 'unknown'.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: The module likely implements caching to satisfy this requirement (since the external API is non-deterministic). Unit tests with a mocked API verify caching logic: call the module twice with the same document, assert results match, and optionally verify the API was called only once. A mock will not miss caching bugs. However, note: if the requirement implies the real non-deterministic API should somehow always return the same result (no caching), then Agentic testing would be needed. Assume caching is the design.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a fixed label. Invoke the classification function twice with the same document. Assert both results are identical. Optionally verify the mock was called exactly once (proving caching).

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Token consumption is a real-world metric that depends on the actual API's tokenization behavior. Mocking the token count hides the real failure: 'we called the API and it used 500 tokens, but budget is 400.' To verify the module correctly enforces the budget against real token counts, you must call the actual external API with real documents. Unit tests with mocked token counts would pass even if the real API exceeds the budget.
behaviors.test_description: Call the module with a real or representative document against the real external API. Capture the token count from the API response. Assert that tokens_consumed ≤ configured_budget. Repeat with several document sizes to cover the 'typical' case.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is a logic bug in retry handling. Mock the API to return a rate-limit error (429) on the first call and success on the second. Mock time or use a controllable test sleep to verify the wait behavior without actual delays. The module's retry logic, error detection, and wait timing are all deterministic and testable in-process. A mock will not miss retry-logic bugs. However, System testing would verify that the retry strategy actually works against real rate-limiting; Unit catches implementation defects.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a 429 (rate-limit) error on the first invocation, then return a valid label on the second. Mock or control time to verify a delay occurred between attempts. Call the module once and assert it returns the valid label (not an error) and that internally it made exactly two API calls with a wait in between.
